behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real AI vision-model judgment of explicit content; stubs cannot validate classification quality. System-level infra checks are not the core risk here, and Workflow is disqualified by the behavior text focusing on a single model decision path ('An image ... is flagged').
behaviors.test_description: Submit real explicit-image samples to the production-like moderation path using the real vision model, then verify they are flagged and the post state is held-for-review (not published).

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This is directly about model robustness to obfuscation and unicode variants, which is AI output-quality dependent. Workflow is disqualified because the behavior centers on one classifier decision, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run a curated corpus of obfuscated hate-speech text variants through the real text moderation model and assert they are flagged/held across obfuscation patterns.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly requires correct handoff across multiple AI-gated stages ('passes both content checks' -> publish decision). Per the integration brake, orchestration across LLM/AI-involved stages should not remain Integration when multiple model decisions are sequenced.
behaviors.test_description: Submit multimodal posts expected to pass both real checks, verify sequential execution of image check then text check, and confirm round-trip observable published state (not held/dropped).

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end SLA on a multi-stage agentic pipeline ('image check, text check, publish decision'), requiring real sequencing and timing across stages. Lower levels miss combined latency and inter-stage effects.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions through the real moderation stack under representative peak load, measure submission-to-final-decision latency, and assert p95 (or required percentile) is <= 5 seconds.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: This is real external error-path behavior (model unavailable) and queue side-effect verification, matching the System triad (external error contract + visible side effect). Agentic is disqualified because correctness does not depend on model output content, only outage handling.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real vision-service unavailability (e.g., timeout/503) in a production-like environment, submit image posts, and verify they are persisted to retry queue and never auto-approved.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: The requirement evaluates consistency across repeated real AI decisions over time (multiple submissions), i.e., multiple agentic steps in sequence. Agentic-only single-call checks are insufficient to catch temporal inconsistency behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the configured short window via the real service, capture all decisions, and assert consistency policy (identical outcome or policy-defined bounded variance) is met.
